questions.q: Which Mauryan emperor fought the Kalinga war?
questions.explanation: The Kalinga war was waged by the Mauryan emperor Ashoka against the kingdom of Kalinga.

questions.q: Around which year is the Kalinga war conventionally dated?
questions.explanation: The Kalinga war is usually dated to around 261 BCE, about eight years into Ashoka's reign.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was the kingdom of Kalinga located?
questions.explanation: Kalinga lay mainly in the coastal region of present-day Odisha.

questions.q: What was the most famous consequence of the Kalinga war for Ashoka personally?
options: He abdicated and became a Jain monk
He embraced Buddhism and renounced war
questions.explanation: Stricken with remorse, Ashoka turned to Buddhism and a policy of non-violence after the carnage of Kalinga.

questions.q: How is the Kalinga war chiefly described in Ashoka's own records?
options: As a glorious triumph to be celebrated
With deep remorse over the bloodshed
As a defeat for the Mauryas
questions.explanation: Ashoka's edicts express sorrow and remorse at the suffering and slaughter caused by the conquest of Kalinga.

questions.q: The Kalinga war marked Ashoka's turn toward which value?
questions.explanation: After Kalinga, Ashoka championed non-violence and ethical conduct rather than conquest by the sword.

questions.q: To which empire did Ashoka add Kalinga as a result of the war?
questions.explanation: Victory in the war brought Kalinga into the Mauryan Empire ruled by Ashoka.

questions.q: Which body of inscriptions is the chief source recording Ashoka's feelings about the Kalinga war?
questions.explanation: Ashoka's rock edicts, especially the thirteenth, are the principal source for his remorse over Kalinga.

questions.q: The Kalinga war was fought in which century BCE?
questions.explanation: The war took place around 261 BCE, in the 3rd century BCE, during Ashoka's reign.

questions.q: What broad character did the Kalinga campaign have, according to historical accounts?
options: It was won without any fighting
It was a long naval blockade
It ended in a peaceful treaty before battle
It was enormously destructive and bloody
questions.explanation: The Kalinga campaign was extremely destructive, with great loss of life that deeply affected Ashoka.

questions.q: Which of Ashoka's Major Rock Edicts gives the most detailed account of the Kalinga war and his remorse?
questions.explanation: Major Rock Edict XIII contains Ashoka's famous account of the Kalinga war and his subsequent change of heart.

questions.q: According to Rock Edict XIII, roughly how many people were said to have been deported as captives from Kalinga?
questions.explanation: Rock Edict XIII states that about 150,000 people were carried away as captives in the Kalinga war.

questions.q: According to Ashoka's edict, roughly how many people were said to have been killed in the Kalinga war?
questions.explanation: Rock Edict XIII records that about 100,000 were slain and many more died in the aftermath of the Kalinga war.

questions.q: Roughly how many years after his consecration did Ashoka conduct the Kalinga war, per his edicts?
questions.explanation: Ashoka's edicts say the Kalinga conquest came about eight years after his consecration as king.

questions.q: At which site near Bhubaneswar in Odisha are Ashokan rock edicts relating to Kalinga found, beside a rock elephant?
questions.explanation: Dhauli, near Bhubaneswar, bears one of the Separate Rock Edicts and is famous for its carved rock elephant.

questions.q: Which other Odisha site, besides Dhauli, contains a 'Separate Rock Edict' addressed to the people of the conquered land?
questions.explanation: Jaugada in southern Odisha carries the second of the Separate Rock Edicts addressed to the Kalinga officials and people.

questions.q: What policy did Ashoka adopt toward conquest after the Kalinga war?
options: He replaced conquest by war with conquest by dhamma
He launched new wars in the south
He demanded annual tribute by force
questions.explanation: Ashoka declared that he now valued 'conquest by dhamma' over conquest by arms after Kalinga.

questions.q: Why is Kalinga thought to have been a particularly valuable region for the Mauryas to control?
options: Its gold mines in the hills
Its coastline and trade routes to Southeast Asia
Its position blocking the Khyber Pass
Its proximity to the Persian frontier
questions.explanation: Kalinga's eastern coastline gave access to maritime trade routes toward Southeast Asia, making it strategically valuable.

questions.q: Before Ashoka's conquest, Kalinga is notable in Mauryan history for what reason?
options: It was the original Mauryan homeland
It had remained outside Mauryan control
questions.explanation: Kalinga had stayed independent of the Mauryas, which is partly why Ashoka launched a campaign to subdue it.

questions.q: The Kalinga war is significant in world history mainly as an early documented case of what?
options: The first use of war elephants
The first naval battle in India
A ruler renouncing war out of conscience
questions.explanation: The war is famous as a rare instance of a victorious king publicly renouncing future warfare on moral grounds.

questions.q: Which river region of Odisha is traditionally associated with the heartland of ancient Kalinga?
questions.explanation: The Mahanadi river and its delta lay at the heart of the ancient region of Kalinga in Odisha.

questions.q: Notably, the two Separate Rock Edicts of Kalinga omit which subject found in Ashoka's main edict series?
options: The account of the Kalinga war itself
questions.explanation: The Separate Rock Edicts at Dhauli and Jaugada tactfully omit the description of the war and replace Edict XIII, addressing the people directly instead.

questions.q: In the Separate Rock Edicts addressed to Kalinga, Ashoka famously tells his officials to treat the people as what?
questions.explanation: In the Kalinga edicts Ashoka instructs his administrators that 'all men are my children,' urging just and paternal rule.

questions.q: A later inscription claims a king of Kalinga reasserted independence and even attacked Magadha; what is that record called?
options: The Aihole inscription of Pulakeshin II
questions.explanation: The Hathigumpha inscription of King Kharavela records a resurgent independent Kalinga in the centuries after Mauryan decline.

questions.q: Which feature distinguishes the Dhauli rock edict site as a monument of the Kalinga conquest?
options: A pair of giant Buddha statues
A sculpted elephant carved from the living rock
questions.explanation: At Dhauli the edict is accompanied by an elephant sculpted out of the rock, among the earliest Mauryan rock-cut sculptures.

questions.q: In Rock Edict XIII, Ashoka expresses that even a fraction of the suffering of Kalinga would be considered what?
options: Grievous and a matter of profound regret
questions.explanation: Ashoka writes that even a hundredth or thousandth part of those killed or deported would now weigh heavily on his conscience.

questions.q: Beyond those killed and captured, Rock Edict XIII also laments deaths from what additional causes during the war?
options: Disease and famine following the fighting
questions.explanation: Edict XIII notes that many more than the slain perished from the disease, hunger, and hardship that followed the war.

questions.q: How long after the Kalinga war did Ashoka typically date his inscriptions, reflecting his reformed outlook?
options: From the date of the Kalinga victory
From his regnal years after consecration
From the founding of the Mauryan Empire
From the death of the Buddha
questions.explanation: Ashoka dated his edicts by regnal years counted from his consecration, framing the post-Kalinga period as his era of dhamma.

questions.q: The Kalinga war effectively completed Mauryan control over which part of the subcontinent's coast?
questions.explanation: Annexing Kalinga secured the Mauryan hold over much of the eastern coastline along the Bay of Bengal.

questions.q: Which feature of the Kalinga edicts shows Ashoka's concern for ongoing fair administration of the region?
options: Orders for periodic tours of inspection by officials
The relocation of Kalinga's capital to Pataliputra
questions.explanation: The Separate Edicts direct that officials make regular tours to ensure the people of Kalinga are treated justly.
